raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A section review centrally includes assessing whether the thesis and contribution framing are coherent, especially given the shift from DialogueDev to RSP.","R1.2":"Logical consistency is load-bearing because the artifact contains potentially conflicting claims about the protocol, prompting strategy, and source of gains.","R1.3":"The review should check whether reported benchmark numbers and configuration comparisons support the claims made from them.","R1.5":"A section-level review should consider whether the ordering and narrative arc make the contribution understandable.","R1.6":"Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register are expected parts of reviewing a paper section.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review was intended by the terse delegation.","RX.2":"The projection should avoid expanding the delegation into unsupported responsibilities such as a full literature audit.","RX.3":"The output must stay within responsibility projection and not perform the requested review itself.","RX.4":"Review responsibilities should be bounded to avoid encouraging unsupported claims or misleading research presentation.","RX.5":"The rationale should trace each active responsibility to the delegation and artifact features."}}

rationale.R1.1: A section review centrally includes assessing whether the thesis and contribution framing are coherent, especially given the shift from DialogueDev to RSP.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is load-bearing because the artifact contains potentially conflicting claims about the protocol, prompting strategy, and source of gains.
rationale.R1.3: The review should check whether reported benchmark numbers and configuration comparisons support the claims made from them.
rationale.R1.5: A section-level review should consider whether the ordering and narrative arc make the contribution understandable.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register are expected parts of reviewing a paper section.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review was intended by the terse delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The projection should avoid expanding the delegation into unsupported responsibilities such as a full literature audit.
rationale.RX.3: The output must stay within responsibility projection and not perform the requested review itself.
rationale.RX.4: Review responsibilities should be bounded to avoid encouraging unsupported claims or misleading research presentation.
rationale.RX.5: The rationale should trace each active responsibility to the delegation and artifact features.
